title: Reading and Use of English – Part 3
description: For questions 17–24, read the text below. Use the word given in CAPITALS at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.

content.text: Modern art has often been a source of (0) CONTROVERSY (CONTROVERSIAL) among critics and the public alike. While some view abstract pieces as (17) MEANINGLESS (MEAN), others see them as a profound expression of the human condition. The (18) REJECTION (REJECT) of traditional techniques in the early 20th century allowed artists to explore new (19) POSSIBILITIES (POSSIBLE). This movement was (20) CHARACTERIZED (CHARACTER) by a desire to challenge the status quo and provoke thought. Many galleries now focus on the (21) EXHIBITION (EXHIBIT) of digital works that incorporate interactive elements. These (22) INNOVATIONS (INNOVATE) have made art more accessible to a (23) BROADLY (BROAD) diverse audience. The (24) SIGNIFICANCE (SIGNIFY) of modern art lies in its ability to reflect the complexities of contemporary life. By breaking away from the constraints of realism, artists can communicate emotions and ideas that are often difficult to put into words, fostering a deeper (25) UNDERSTANDING (UNDERSTAND) of our rapidly changing world and the diverse cultures that inhabit it.

example.text: Modern art has often been a source of (0) CONTROVERSY (CONTROVERSIAL)...
example.explanation: Noun required after the preposition 'of'; CONTROVERSIAL → CONTROVERSY.

questions.explanation: Adjective required to describe the 'pieces'; MEAN → MEANINGLESS.
Noun required after the definite article; REJECT → REJECTION.
Plural noun required after 'new'; POSSIBLE → POSSIBILITIES.
Past participle required for the passive voice; CHARACTER → CHARACTERIZED.
Plural noun required as the subject; INNOVATE → INNOVATIONS.
Adverb required to modify the adjective 'diverse'; BROAD → BROADLY.
Noun required after the definite article; SIGNIFY → SIGNIFICANCE.
